# Kubernetes Recipes

> Complete index of 721+ production-ready Kubernetes guides by Luca Berton.

## Ai

- [AI Content Watermarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-content-watermarking-kubernetes/): Deploy AI-generated content watermarking on Kubernetes. Invisible watermarks, SynthID integration, detection APIs, image and text watermarking pipelines.
- [AI Infrastructure Cost Optimization on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-infrastructure-cost-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes. GPU sharing, spot instances, inference batching, model quantization, token economics, and right-sizing GPU workloads.
- [AI Model Storage: hostPath vs PVC for Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/model-storage-hostpath-pvc/): Deploy AI models on Kubernetes using hostPath and PVC storage. Compare performance, security trade-offs, and production patterns for model serving.
- [AI Supercomputing on Kubernetes GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-supercomputing-kubernetes-gpu-clusters/): Build AI supercomputing platforms on Kubernetes. Multi-node GPU training, NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, InfiniBand RDMA, NCCL tuning, Blackwell clusters, and job scheduling.
- [AI-Native Development Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-native-development-kubernetes/): Build AI-native development platforms on Kubernetes. AI coding agents, automated testing, Copilot infrastructure, dev containers, and AI-driven CI/CD pipelines.
- [AIPerf Benchmark LLMs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-benchmark-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA AIPerf to benchmark LLM inference performance on Kubernetes. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput with real-time dashboard and GPU telemetry.
- [AIPerf Concurrency Sweep on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-concurrency-sweep-kubernetes/): Run AIPerf concurrency sweeps on Kubernetes to find optimal LLM serving capacity. Automate 1-128 concurrent user benchmarks with batch Jobs.
- [AIPerf Goodput and SLO Benchmarks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-goodput-slo-benchmark/): Measure LLM goodput with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Define SLOs for TTFT and ITL, calculate effective throughput, and benchmark with timeslice analysis.
- [AIPerf Multi-Model Benchmark on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-multi-model-benchmark/): Compare multiple LLM models and backends with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Benchmark vLLM vs TGI vs Triton with automated multi-run confidence intervals.
- [AIPerf Trace Replay Benchmarks on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-trace-replay-kubernetes/): Replay production traffic traces with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Use moon_cake format, ShareGPT datasets, and fixed schedules for realistic LLM benchmarks.
- [Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/agentic-ai-multiagent-kubernetes/): Deploy autonomous AI agents and multi-agent orchestration on Kubernetes. LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, tool-calling agents, agent-to-agent communication, and scaling patterns.
- [Autonomous Industrial Systems on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/autonomous-industrial-systems-kubernetes/): Orchestrate autonomous factories and logistics with Kubernetes. Digital twins, robot fleet coordination, industrial IoT pipelines, predictive maintenance, and OPC UA integration.
- [Autoscale LLM Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-autoscaling-kubernetes/): Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaling and KEDA for LLM workloads using GPU utilization, request queue depth, and custom metrics.
- [Batch AI Workloads with Volcano Scheduler on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-batch-processing-volcano/): Schedule and manage batch AI training and inference jobs using Volcano scheduler with gang scheduling, fair-share queues, job plugins, and preemption on.
- [Batch Scheduling with PodGroups in KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-podgroups/): Implement gang scheduling for distributed training jobs using KAI Scheduler PodGroups to ensure all-or-nothing pod scheduling
- [Benchmark NCCL AllReduce Performance on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-allreduce-benchmark-profile/): Measure NCCL AllReduce bandwidth and latency on Kubernetes to validate distributed training network performance across multi-GPU clusters.
- [Build a RAG Pipeline on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/rag-pipeline-kubernetes/): Deploy a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline on Kubernetes using a vector database, embedding model, and LLM inference server.
- [Compare NCCL Intra-Node vs Inter-Node Performance](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/compare-nccl-intra-inter-node/): Build a repeatable comparison between local and cross-node NCCL throughput to validate GPU cluster interconnect scaling and identify bottlenecks early.
- [Copy NVIDIA NIM Images to Internal Quay Registry](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/copy-nim-image-internal-quay-registry/): Pull NIM container images from nvcr.io and push to an internal Quay registry. Covers authentication, tagging, air-gapped workflows, and curl token issues.
- [Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU Node Setup](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dell-poweredge-xe7740-gpu-setup/): Configure Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU nodes with H200 GPUs for OpenShift and Kubernetes including BIOS, power, cooling, and network setup.
- [Deploy Fish Audio TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-fish-audio-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Fish Audio S2-Pro 5B text-to-speech model on Kubernetes for high-quality voice synthesis with multi-speaker support and streaming audio.
- [Deploy GLM-5 754B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-glm5-754b-kubernetes/): Deploy Zhipu AI GLM-5 754B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. One of the largest open-weight models with multi-node tensor parallelism across 8+ GPUs.
- [Deploy Granite 4.0 Speech on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-granite-speech-kubernetes/): Deploy IBM Granite 4.0 1B Speech model on Kubernetes for automatic speech recognition. Lightweight 2B model runs on CPU or small GPU for STT workloads.
- [Deploy Kimi K2.5 1.1T MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-kimi-k25-kubernetes/): Deploy Moonshot AI Kimi-K2.5 1.1T MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. The largest open MoE model with 2.69M downloads for frontier AI tasks.
- [Deploy LTX Video Generation on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-ltx-video-kubernetes/): Deploy Lightricks LTX-2.3 image-to-video model on Kubernetes for AI video generation with batch processing and S3 output storage.
- [Deploy Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama2-70b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes with multi-GPU tensor parallelism, vLLM serving, and production-ready health checks and resource limits.
- [Deploy Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama31-8b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on Kubernetes with vLLM. Production-ready single-GPU deployment with 128K context, tool calling, and autoscaling.
- [Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 small language model on Kubernetes with vLLM. Efficient 14B model with GPT-4 level reasoning on a single GPU.
- [Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-minimax-m25-kubernetes/): Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. High-performance LLM with 485K downloads, optimized for multi-turn conversation and long context.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with NVIDIA NIM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-nvidia-nim/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B using NVIDIA NIM with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized GPU inference.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with vLLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-vllm-kubernetes/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B-v0.1 using vLLM as an OpenAI-compatible inference server on Kubernetes with GPU fractioning.
- [Deploy Multinode NIM Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-multinode-nim-models-kubernetes/): Run large language models across multiple GPU nodes with NVIDIA NIM. Tensor parallelism, NCCL, InfiniBand, and Kubernetes Job orchestration.
- [Deploy NVIDIA Clara on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-clara-medical-ai-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Clara medical AI and drug discovery platform on Kubernetes. Run digital biology and medtech inference workloads with GPU acceleration.
- [Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron 120B MoE on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-nemotron-120b-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B MoE model on Kubernetes. 120B total parameters with 12B active for enterprise-grade inference.
- [Deploy Phi-4 Reasoning Vision on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-reasoning-vision-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B on Kubernetes for multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning with visual understanding on a single GPU.
- [Deploy Qwen3 235B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-235b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3-235B-A22B mixture-of-experts model on Kubernetes. Only 22B parameters active per token for efficient 235B-class inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3 Coder 80B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-coder-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B on Kubernetes for code generation, review, and refactoring. Production-ready AI coding assistant with multi-GPU serving.
- [Deploy Qwen3 TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice on Kubernetes for text-to-speech with custom voice cloning. 1.13M downloads, lightweight single-GPU deployment.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 35B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-35b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-35B-A3B mixture-of-experts multimodal model on Kubernetes. 35B total parameters with only 3B active for ultra-efficient inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 397B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-397b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-397B-A17B MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. 397B total parameters with only 17B active per token for frontier VLM inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 9B Multimodal on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-9b-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-9B vision-language model on Kubernetes with vLLM. Process images and text with a single GPU deployment.
- [Deploy Sarvam 105B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-sarvam-105b-kubernetes/): Deploy Sarvam 105B multilingual LLM on Kubernetes with vLLM. India's largest open language model with native support for 10+ Indic languages.
- [Deploy Whisper Speech-to-Text on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-whisper-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text on Kubernetes with faster-whisper, batch transcription Jobs, and real-time streaming endpoints.
- [Deploying Vector Databases on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vector-database-kubernetes/): Deploy and operate vector databases (Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant) on Kubernetes for RAG pipelines, semantic search, and AI applications with persistent.
- [Distributed Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed LLM inference with tensor parallelism across multiple GPUs and pipeline parallelism across nodes on Kubernetes.
- [Distributed Inference on Kubernetes with Run:ai](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-runai-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed AI inference with NVIDIA Run:ai on Kubernetes. Single-node Knative, multinode LeaderWorkerSet, NIM, autoscaling, and observability.
- [Distributed Training with Kubeflow Training Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-distributed-training/): Run multi-node distributed PyTorch and TensorFlow training jobs using Kubeflow Training Operator with NCCL, RDMA, and shared storage.
- [Domain-Specific Language Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/domain-specific-language-models-kubernetes/): Deploy and fine-tune domain-specific LLMs on Kubernetes. Legal, healthcare, finance, and code models with LoRA fine-tuning, NIM serving, and RAG pipelines.
- [Dynamic Resource Allocation for GPUs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-dynamic-resource-allocation-gpu/): Use Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation to schedule GPUs. DRA ResourceClaims, partitionable devices, GPU sharing, and structured parameters for accelerators.
- [Enable GPUDirect Storage in ClusterPolicy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-clusterpolicy-gds-enable/): Enable NVIDIA GPUDirect Storage (GDS) in the GPU Operator ClusterPolicy for direct GPU-to-NVMe data paths. Driver module configuration and verification.
- [GPU Sharing and Bin Packing with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-gpu-sharing/): Maximize GPU utilization with KAI Scheduler GPU sharing, fractional GPUs, and bin packing strategies for Kubernetes AI workloads.
- [GPU Sharing with MPS and MIG on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-sharing-mps-mig/): Share NVIDIA GPUs across multiple pods using MPS time-slicing and MIG hardware partitioning. Maximize GPU utilization for inference workloads.
- [GPU Time-Slicing on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-time-slicing-kubernetes/): Share GPUs across multiple workloads using NVIDIA time-slicing on Kubernetes. Configure the device plugin, set replica counts, and manage fairness.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark LLM Serving](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmark-llm/): Benchmark LLM inference endpoints with NVIDIA GenAI-Perf for throughput, latency percentiles, time-to-first-token, and ITL metrics.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark Triton on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-triton-benchmark/): Benchmark NVIDIA Triton Inference Server performance on Kubernetes using GenAI-Perf. Measure TTFT, inter-token latency, throughput, and GPU telemetry.
- [GitOps for AI Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gitops-ai-workloads-kubernetes/): Deploy AI models with GitOps on Kubernetes. Version ML models in Git, ArgoCD for model rollouts, Flux for GPU cluster sync, and reproducible inference pipelines.
- [Hierarchical Queues and Resource Fairness with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-queues/): Configure hierarchical queues in KAI Scheduler for multi-tenant GPU clusters with quotas, limits, and Dominant Resource Fairness (DRF)
- [Install NVIDIA GPU Operator on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-gpu-operator-install/): Deploy the NVIDIA GPU Operator to automate GPU driver, container toolkit, and device plugin management across your Kubernetes cluster.
- [Installing NVIDIA KAI Scheduler for AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-installation/): Deploy KAI Scheduler for optimized GPU resource allocation in Kubernetes AI/ML clusters with hierarchical queues and batch scheduling
- [Kubeflow Operator: Full ML Platform on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-operator-platform/): Deploy the complete Kubeflow platform on Kubernetes with the Kubeflow Operator. Covers Pipelines, Notebooks, KServe, Katib, and multi-tenant ML workflows.
- [Kubeflow Training Operator on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-training-operator/): Install Kubeflow Training Operator for distributed ML training with PyTorchJob, TFJob, and MPIJob on GPU-enabled Kubernetes clusters.
- [Kubernetes AI Gateway: Inference Extension Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-gateway-inference-extension/): Use the Kubernetes AI Gateway and Inference Extension to route LLM traffic. Model-aware routing, load balancing across inference backends, and Gateway API for AI workloads.
- [Kubernetes LLM Serving Frameworks Compared](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-serving-frameworks-compared/): Compare vLLM, NVIDIA NIM, Triton, Ollama, and llama.cpp for serving LLMs on Kubernetes — features, performance, and when to use each.
- [Kueue for Batch Jobs and GPU Queues on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kueue-batch-gpu-jobs-kubernetes/): Use Kueue to manage batch job queues on Kubernetes. GPU quota, fair sharing, priority queues, ML training workloads, and multi-tenant cluster scheduling.
- [LeaderWorkerSet Operator for AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/leaderworkerset-operator/): Deploy distributed AI training with LeaderWorkerSet Operator on Kubernetes and OpenShift for leader-worker topology with gang scheduling.
- [Llama 2 70B Model Size and GPU Requirements](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llama-70b-model-size-gpu-requirements/): Llama 2 70B FP16 requires 140GB VRAM. GPU sizing for FP16, FP8, INT4 quantization. Multi-GPU setups with A100, H100, H200 for Kubernetes inference deployment.
- [Llama Stack on Kubernetes with NVIDIA NIM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llama-stack-kubernetes-nim/): Deploy Meta Llama Stack on Kubernetes for unified inference, RAG, agents, and safety APIs using NVIDIA NIM as the inference backend.
- [MLPerf Benchmarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/mlperf-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run MLPerf inference and training benchmarks on Kubernetes GPU clusters to validate AI workload performance and compare hardware configurations.
- [MPI Operator for Distributed Training](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/mpi-operator-kubernetes/): Deploy MPI Operator on Kubernetes for distributed GPU training with Horovod and NCCL. Run multi-node MPI jobs natively in Kubernetes pods.
- [Multi-GPU and Tensor Parallel LLM Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/multi-gpu-llm-inference/): Deploy large language models across multiple GPUs using tensor parallelism with vLLM and NVIDIA NIM on Kubernetes for high-throughput inference serving.
- [NIM LLM Support Matrix and GPU Compatibility](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-support-matrix-gpu-compatibility/): Complete NVIDIA NIM support matrix for Kubernetes. Supported models, profiles, precision formats, GPU compatibility, and hardware requirements per model.
- [NIM Model Profiles and Selection on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-model-profiles-selection-kubernetes/): Configure NIM_MODEL_PROFILE for NVIDIA NIM deployments on Kubernetes. List profiles, select by ID or name, tune VRAM, and override with vLLM CLI args.
- [NIM Multi-Node Deployment with Helm on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-multinode-deployment-helm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA NIM across multiple Kubernetes nodes using Helm, LeaderWorkerSet, Ray, and vLLM. Run Llama 405B and DeepSeek-R1 on 16+ GPUs.
- [NVIDIA Dynamo Distributed Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-dynamo-distributed-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Dynamo on Kubernetes for disaggregated LLM inference. KV-aware routing, prefill/decode splitting, Grove operator, and zero-config deployment.
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator Setup on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-gpu-operator-setup/): Install and configure NVIDIA GPU Operator on Kubernetes. Driver containers, toolkit, device plugin, DCGM monitoring, and ClusterPolicy setup.
- [NVIDIA H200 GPU Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-h200-gpu-kubernetes/): Deploy and optimize AI workloads on NVIDIA H200 GPUs with 141GB HBM3e memory for large model inference and training on Kubernetes.
- [NVIDIA H300 GPU Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-h300-gpu-kubernetes/): Prepare for NVIDIA H300 Blackwell-Next GPUs on Kubernetes with next-gen HBM3e memory, NVLink 5.0, and FP4 inference capabilities.
- [NVIDIA NeMo Training on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-nemo-training-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA NeMo framework on Kubernetes for large language model pre-training, fine-tuning, and RLHF with multi-node GPU clusters.
- [NVIDIA Open GPU + GPUDirect RDMA + DOCA-OFED + SR-IOV Stack](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-open-gpu-dma-buf-gpudirect-rdma-doca-sriov/): Deploy NVIDIA AI networking on Kubernetes: Open GPU driver with DMA-BUF, GPUDirect RDMA, DOCA-OFED, and SR-IOV VF isolation.
- [NVIDIA Pyxis and Enroot for SLURM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-pyxis-slurm-containers/): Use NVIDIA Pyxis and Enroot to run GPU containers in SLURM jobs. Bridge SLURM HPC scheduling with container-native AI workloads and NGC images.
- [Node Feature Discovery Operator for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/node-feature-discovery-operator/): Install and configure Node Feature Discovery (NFD) Operator to auto-detect hardware features like GPUs, NICs, CPU flags, and USB devices on Kubernetes nodes.
- [Physical AI and Robotics Orchestration on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/physical-ai-robotics-kubernetes/): Orchestrate physical AI and robotics fleets with Kubernetes. ROS 2 on K8s, robot fleet management, edge-cloud hybrid, NVIDIA Isaac, and autonomous system deployment.
- [Quantize LLMs for Efficient GPU Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-quantization-kubernetes/): Run quantized LLM models (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF) on Kubernetes to reduce GPU memory requirements and serve models on smaller GPUs.
- [Quantum Computing on Kubernetes: Hybrid Workflows](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/quantum-computing-kubernetes-integration/): Run quantum computing workloads on Kubernetes. Qiskit, Cirq, PennyLane hybrid classical-quantum pipelines, quantum job scheduling, and QPU integration patterns.
- [Rebuild NIM with Custom Model on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/rebuild-nim-custom-model-kubernetes/): Step-by-step guide to deploy custom, fine-tuned, or self-hosted models with NVIDIA NIM on Kubernetes. Model-free NIM from HuggingFace, S3, NGC, or local path.
- [RetinaNet Object Detection on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-retinanet-kubernetes/): Deploy RetinaNet object detection model on Kubernetes with Triton Inference Server, TensorRT optimization, and batch processing pipelines.
- [Run NCCL AllGather Benchmarks for Model Parallel Validation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-allgather-benchmark-profile/): Use all-gather NCCL tests to evaluate GPU communication behavior and throughput for tensor-parallel and model-parallel distributed AI workloads on Kubernetes.
- [Run NCCL Tests on Kubernetes for GPU Network Validation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/run-nccl-tests-kubernetes/): Benchmark GPU-to-GPU communication using NVIDIA nccl-tests on Kubernetes or OpenShift to validate bandwidth and latency.
- [Run:AI GPU Quotas on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-gpu-quotas-openshift/): Configure Run:AI scheduler quotas for fair GPU sharing with guaranteed, over-quota borrowing, and per-tenant GPU allocation policies.
- [Run:ai + Dynamo Multi-Node Scheduling on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-dynamo-multinode-scheduling-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Dynamo with Run:ai v2.23 for gang scheduling and topology-aware placement. Atomic pod launches, zone co-location, and disaggregated inference.
- [Run:ai NIM Distributed Inference Tutorial](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-nim-distributed-inference-tutorial/): Step-by-step guide to deploy DeepSeek-R1 distributed inference on Run:ai with LeaderWorkerSet, SGLang, PVC caching, and OpenShift security.
- [Run:ai Topology-Aware Scheduling Deep Dive](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-topology-aware-scheduling-kubernetes/): Configure Run:ai topology-aware scheduling for distributed AI workloads. Multi-level hierarchies, required vs preferred placement, LeaderWorkerSet, and GB200 NVLink domains.
- [SLURM and Kubernetes Integration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/slurm-kubernetes-integration/): Integrate SLURM HPC workload manager with Kubernetes for hybrid AI and scientific computing. Bridge HPC batch scheduling with container orchestration.
- [Shared Model Caching Across Pods on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/model-caching-shared-memory/): Optimize LLM inference startup and reduce storage costs by sharing model weights across pods using emptyDir, hostPath, ReadWriteMany PVCs, and init.
- [Stable Diffusion XL on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-stable-diffusion-xl-kubernetes/): Deploy Stable Diffusion XL for image generation on Kubernetes with TensorRT acceleration, queued batch processing, and S3 output storage.
- [TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM on Triton](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-vllm-comparison/): Compare TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends on Triton Inference Server. When to use each, performance benchmarks, and migration strategies.
- [Test LLM Inference Endpoints with curl](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/test-llm-inference-endpoints/): Validate Kubernetes-hosted LLM inference services using curl against OpenAI-compatible /v1/models, /v1/completions, and /v1/chat/completions endpoints.
- [Time-Slicing vs MIG vs Full GPU Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/timeslicing-mig-full-gpu/): Compare GPU sharing strategies: time-slicing for notebooks, MIG for isolated inference, and full GPU for training workloads.
- [Topology-Aware Scheduling with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-topology-aware/): Optimize GPU workload placement using KAI Scheduler's Topology-Aware Scheduling (TAS) for NVLink, NVSwitch, and disaggregated serving architectures
- [Triton Autoscaling with GPU Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-autoscaling-gpu-metrics/): Autoscale Triton Inference Server on Kubernetes using GPU utilization, request queue depth, and inference latency metrics with KEDA and HPA.
- [Triton Multi-Model Serving on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-multi-model-serving/): Serve multiple LLMs simultaneously on Triton Inference Server using TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends with model routing and GPU scheduling.
- [Triton TensorRT-LLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized large language model serving with GPU acceleration.
- [Triton with vLLM Backend on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-vllm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with vLLM backend on Kubernetes for flexible LLM serving with PagedAttention and continuous batching.

## Autoscaling

- [Advanced Cluster Autoscaler Configuration and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cluster-autoscaler-advanced/): Fine-tune the Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler with expanders, priority-based scaling, mixed instance policies, and GPU node pool autoscaling for production clusters.
- [Custom Metrics Autoscaling in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-custom-metrics-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes pods on custom application metrics with Prometheus Adapter. Configure HPA with custom and external metrics beyond CPU and memory.
- [Goldilocks: VPA Recommendations Dashboard](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-goldilocks-vpa-dashboard/): Deploy Goldilocks to visualize VPA recommendations across namespaces. Right-size Kubernetes resource requests and limits with a web dashboard.
- [HPA in Kubernetes: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/horizontal-pod-autoscaler/): HPA in Kubernetes explained with YAML examples. Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for CPU, memory, and custom metrics with scaling policies and VPA comparison.
- [How to Configure Cluster Autoscaler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler/): Automatically scale your Kubernetes cluster nodes based on workload demand. Learn to configure Cluster Autoscaler for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
- [How to Scale Based on Custom Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/hpa-custom-metrics/): Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with custom and external metrics. Learn to scale on application-specific metrics like queue depth and request latency.
- [How to Use KEDA for Event-Driven Autoscaling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/keda-event-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes workloads based on external events with KEDA. Configure scalers for queues, databases, and custom metrics beyond CPU/memory.
- [KEDA vs HPA: Event-Driven Autoscaling Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/keda-vs-hpa-autoscaling-comparison/): Compare KEDA and HPA for Kubernetes autoscaling. Scale on Kafka lag, Prometheus metrics, queue depth, cron, and custom events. KEDA vs HPA decision guide.
- [KEDA: Event-Driven Autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/keda-event-driven-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes workloads with KEDA based on external events: queue depth, cron schedules, Prometheus metrics, HTTP traffic, and 60+ event sources.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/karpenter-node-autoscaling/): Replace Cluster Autoscaler with Karpenter for faster, smarter node provisioning. Right-sized instances, spot fallback, consolidation, and GPU-aware scaling.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaling for Kubernetes on AWS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/karpenter-node-autoscaling-kubernetes/): Deploy Karpenter for fast, flexible node autoscaling on AWS EKS. Configure NodePools, EC2NodeClasses, consolidation, and GPU provisioning with sub-minute node startup.
- [Kubernetes Capacity Planning for Enterprise Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-enterprise-capacity-planning/): Right-size enterprise clusters with data-driven capacity planning. Forecast resource needs, optimize bin-packing, and plan for growth with metrics.
- [Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler-setup/): Configure the Cluster Autoscaler to automatically add and remove nodes based on pod scheduling demands. Covers AWS, GKE, Azure, and bare-metal setups.
- [Kubernetes Cost Optimization Strategies](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cost-optimization-strategies/): Reduce Kubernetes cloud costs by 30-60 percent. Covers right-sizing, spot instances, cluster autoscaler tuning, resource quotas, and FinOps practices.
- [OpenClaw Auto-Scaling with KEDA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/openclaw-autoscaling-keda/): Scale OpenClaw agents based on message queue depth using KEDA event-driven autoscaling for Discord, Telegram, and Slack.
- [OpenClaw Resource Limits and Tuning on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/openclaw-resource-limits-tuning/): Size CPU, memory, and storage for OpenClaw on Kubernetes. Tuning profiles for light usage, browser automation, and production deployments.
- [Optimize Kubernetes Resource Usage](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-resource-optimization/): Right-size pods with VPA, optimize with Goldilocks, implement request-to-limit ratios, QoS classes, and cost-aware management.
- [Vertical Pod Autoscaler VPA Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/vertical-pod-autoscaler/): Install and configure Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscaler. VPA updateMode Off, Initial, Auto, recommendations, hack/vpa-up.sh install, and HPA coexistence.
- [Virtual Kubelet for Serverless Kubernetes Scaling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/virtual-kubelet-serverless-scaling/): Deploy Virtual Kubelet to burst Kubernetes workloads to serverless backends like Azure ACI, AWS Fargate, and Hashicorp Nomad for infinite scaling.

## Configuration

- [400 Recipes Milestone: What We Built and What's Next](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-recipes-400-pages-milestone/): Kubernetes Recipes reaches 400 articles. Explore new AI/GPU infrastructure, NVIDIA networking, ArgoCD GitOps, OpenShift, and RHACS security recipes.
- [Automate MCP Updates with Drain Script](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-update-automation-script/): Bash script to automate OpenShift MachineConfigPool updates when drains are blocked by PDB violations. Auto-detects blockers, scales down, drains, and restores.
- [ClusterPolicy MOFED Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/clusterpolicy-mofed-upgrade/): Configure safe MOFED driver upgrade policies in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy with rolling updates, node draining, and rollback procedures.
- [Configure ClusterPolicy kernelModuleType for GPU Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configure-clusterpolicy-kernel-module-type/): Understand and configure the driver.kernelModuleType field in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy to choose between auto, open, and proprietary kernel.
- [Configure Container Registries via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-registries-conf/): Set up mirror registries and blocked registries on OpenShift nodes using MachineConfig to control CRI-O image pull on RHCOS.
- [Configure MCP maxUnavailable for Rollouts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-maxunavailable-configuration/): Control how many nodes the MachineConfig Operator updates simultaneously. Set maxUnavailable for faster rollouts or safer one-at-a-time updates in production.
- [Configure NTP Chrony via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-chrony-ntp/): Set custom NTP servers on OpenShift RHCOS nodes using MachineConfig. Fix time drift, configure chrony, and verify time synchronization across your cluster.
- [Configure PDBs for OpenShift Routers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-router-pdb-configuration/): Set PodDisruptionBudgets for OpenShift IngressController routers. Balance availability during maintenance with node drain ability.
- [Cordon, Drain, and Uncordon Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-node-cordon-uncordon/): Safely remove workloads from OpenShift and Kubernetes nodes for maintenance. Cordon to prevent scheduling, drain to evict pods, uncordon to restore.
- [Create Custom CatalogSources for OLM Operators](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-catalogsource-custom-operator/): Configure CatalogSource in OpenShift to serve custom operator catalogs from private registries or air-gapped environments.
- [Crossplane for Cloud Infrastructure Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/crossplane-infrastructure-management/): Use Crossplane to provision and manage cloud infrastructure resources like databases, storage, and networking using Kubernetes-native APIs and GitOps.
- [DOCA Driver on OpenShift with DTK](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/doca-driver-openshift-dtk/): Build and deploy precompiled NVIDIA DOCA Driver containers on OpenShift using DriverToolKit, MachineConfig, and upgrade lifecycle.
- [Dynamic Resource Allocation for GPUs with NVIDIA DRA Driver](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-gpu-allocation/): Learn to use Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) for flexible GPU allocation, sharing, and configuration with the NVIDIA DRA Driver
- [Essential Kubernetes Commands Reference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-commands/): configuration
- [Fix ConfigMap Changes Not Applied to Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-hot-reload-troubleshooting/): Debug ConfigMap updates not reflected in running pods. Covers volume mount propagation delays, env var immutability, and sidecar-based reload strategies.
- [GPU Cluster Upgrade Version Matrix](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-upgrade-version-matrix/): Maintain a version compatibility matrix for GPU Operator, Network Operator, drivers, firmware, CUDA, and OpenShift for safe upgrades.
- [GPU Operator ClusterPolicy Complete Reference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-operator-clusterpolicy-reference/): Complete reference for the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy CRD covering driver, toolkit, device plugin, MOFED, GDS, MIG, and DCGM configuration options.
- [GitOps for OpenClaw Workspaces on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-workspace-gitops/): Manage OpenClaw agent workspaces (SOUL.md, skills, memory) with GitOps using Flux or ArgoCD, enabling version-controlled AI persona management on.
- [How ITMS Updates registries.conf via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/itms-registries-conf-machineconfig/): How ITMS and IDMS update /etc/containers/registries.conf on immutable CoreOS nodes via MCO and MachineConfig. Full chain deep-dive.
- [How to Configure Environment Variables and ConfigMaps](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/environment-variables-configmaps/): Manage application configuration with environment variables and ConfigMaps. Learn injection methods, mounting as files, and dynamic configuration updates.
- [How to Configure Image Pull Secrets](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/image-pull-secrets/): Pull container images from private registries using image pull secrets. Configure authentication for Docker Hub, GCR, ECR, ACR, and private registries.
- [How to Configure Pod Resource Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/pod-resource-management/): Set CPU and memory requests and limits effectively. Understand QoS classes, resource quotas, and optimize container resource allocation.
- [How to Configure Resource Limits and Requests](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-limits-requests/): Set CPU and memory requests and limits for containers. Understand QoS classes, resource quotas, and best practices for right-sizing workloads.
- [How to Configure Resource Quotas](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas/): Limit resource consumption per namespace with ResourceQuotas. Control CPU, memory, storage, and object counts to ensure fair cluster sharing.
- [How to Configure Resource Quotas per Namespace](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas-namespace/): Implement resource quotas to limit CPU, memory, and object counts per namespace. Ensure fair resource allocation across teams and environments.
- [How to Create Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/custom-resource-definitions/): Extend Kubernetes API with Custom Resource Definitions. Define custom objects, configure validation schemas, and manage CRD lifecycle.
- [How to Implement Kubernetes Taints and Tolerations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/node-taints-tolerations/): Control pod scheduling with taints and tolerations. Dedicate nodes for specific workloads, handle node conditions, and implement scheduling constraints.
- [How to Manage ConfigMaps and Secrets Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-secrets-management/): Master Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets for application configuration. Learn creation methods, mounting strategies, and security best practices.
- [How to Manage Kubernetes API Versions and Deprecations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/api-versions-deprecations/): Handle Kubernetes API version changes and deprecations. Migrate resources to stable APIs and ensure cluster upgrade compatibility.
- [How to Manage Kubernetes Contexts and Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubeconfig-contexts/): Switch between multiple clusters efficiently. Configure kubeconfig, manage contexts, and set up secure multi-cluster access.
- [How to Manage Kubernetes Namespaces Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/namespace-management/): Master Kubernetes namespace organization for multi-team environments. Learn resource quotas, network policies, and RBAC per namespace.
- [How to Optimize Kubernetes Costs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cost-optimization/): Reduce cloud costs in Kubernetes clusters. Right-size resources, use spot instances, implement autoscaling, and monitor spending effectively.
- [How to Set Resource Requests and Limits Properly](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-requests-limits/): Master Kubernetes resource management with proper CPU and memory requests and limits. Avoid OOMKills, throttling, and resource contention.
- [How to Upgrade Kubernetes Clusters Safely](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cluster-upgrade/): Perform Kubernetes cluster upgrades with zero downtime. Learn upgrade strategies, pre-flight checks, rollback procedures, and best practices for.
- [How to Use Downward API for Pod Metadata](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/downward-api-metadata/): Expose pod and container metadata to applications using the Downward API. Access labels, annotations, resource limits, and node information from within.
- [How to Use Kubernetes Finalizers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers/): Manage resource cleanup with Kubernetes finalizers. Implement custom cleanup logic and understand how finalizers prevent premature resource deletion.
- [How to Use Kubernetes Jobs and CronJobs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-jobs-cronjobs/): Run batch workloads and scheduled tasks with Jobs and CronJobs. Configure retries, parallelism, and completion tracking for reliable task execution.
- [How to Use Kubernetes Lease Objects](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-lease-objects/): Implement leader election and distributed coordination with Kubernetes Lease objects. Build highly available controllers and prevent split-brain scenarios.
- [How to Use Kustomize for Configuration Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kustomize-configuration/): Manage Kubernetes configurations with Kustomize overlays. Customize base manifests for different environments without template duplication.
- [How to Use Labels and Annotations Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-labels-annotations/): Organize and manage Kubernetes resources with labels and annotations. Implement labeling strategies for selection, filtering, and metadata.
- [How to Use Pod Presets and Mutations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/pod-mutation-injection/): Automatically inject configurations into pods using admission controllers. Configure environment variables, volumes, and annotations at deployment time.
- [How to Use the Downward API](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/downward-api/): Expose pod and container metadata to applications using the Downward API. Access labels, annotations, resource limits, and pod information from within.
- [ITMS External-to-External Registry Mirroring](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/itms-external-registry-mapping/): Configure OpenShift ImageTagMirrorSet to map external registries to your private registry. Mirror Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay.io, and NVIDIA NGC.
- [Inspect MachineConfig Annotations on Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-annotation-inspection/): Read and interpret MachineConfig annotations on OpenShift nodes. Check desired vs current config, node state, and rendered config hashes to diagnose MCP issues.

- [Kubernetes API Deprecation Migration Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-api-deprecation-migration/): Migrate deprecated Kubernetes APIs before cluster upgrades. Detect deprecated resources with pluto, kubent, and kubectl convert.
- [Kubernetes Affinity and Anti-Affinity Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-affinity-anti-affinity/): configuration

- [Kubernetes Change Management for Enterprise Operations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-change-management-enterprise/): Implement ITIL-aligned change management for Kubernetes with approval gates, maintenance windows, rollback procedures, and change audit trails.
- [Kubernetes Cluster Upgrade Step-by-Step](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cluster-upgrade-guide/): Upgrade Kubernetes clusters safely with kubeadm. Covers pre-flight checks, control plane upgrade, worker node drain, and rollback procedures.
- [Kubernetes ConfigMap Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-guide/): Create and use ConfigMaps in Kubernetes for application configuration. Mount as files, inject as environment variables, and hot-reload without restarting pods.
- [Kubernetes ConfigMap Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap/): configuration
- [Kubernetes ConfigMap Hot Reload Without Restart](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-hot-reload/): Reload Kubernetes ConfigMaps without pod restarts. Volume-mounted auto-update, Reloader controller, checksum annotations, and inotify-based config watch patterns.
- [Kubernetes Cordon, Drain, and Uncordon Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cordon-drain-node/): configuration
- [Kubernetes CronJob concurrencyPolicy Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cronjob-concurrencypolicy/): Configure Kubernetes CronJob concurrencyPolicy: Allow, Forbid, and Replace. Control overlapping job execution, prevent duplicate runs, and handle slow jobs.
- [Kubernetes Disaster Recovery Planning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-disaster-recovery-plan/): Build a Kubernetes disaster recovery plan with etcd backups, Velero, cross-region replication, and RTO/RPO targets for production clusters.
- [Kubernetes Disaster Recovery for Enterprise](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-disaster-recovery-enterprise/): Kubernetes disaster recovery with Velero backup and restore. Cross-region replication, etcd snapshots, multi-cluster failover, and RTO/RPO strategies.
- [Kubernetes Downward API: Pod Metadata to Env](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-downward-api-pod-metadata/): Use the Kubernetes Downward API to expose pod name, namespace, node name, IP, labels, and annotations as environment variables or volume files in containers.
- [Kubernetes Environment Variables Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-environment-variables/): Set Kubernetes environment variables with envFrom, configMapRef, secretKeyRef, and the Downward API. Variable ordering, fieldRef, and best practices.
- [Kubernetes Finalizers: Prevent Premature Deletion](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers-guide/): How Kubernetes finalizers work to prevent premature resource deletion. Add, remove, and troubleshoot stuck finalizers on PVCs, namespaces, and custom resources.
- [Kubernetes Health Checks Best Practices](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-health-checks/): configuration
- [Kubernetes Init Containers Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-init-containers/): configuration

- [Kubernetes Labels and Selectors Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-labels-selectors-guide/): Master Kubernetes labels and selectors for organizing and querying resources. Label conventions, equality selectors, set-based selectors, and field selectors.
- [Kubernetes Labels and Selectors Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-labels-selectors/): configuration
- [Kubernetes Lease API for Leader Election](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-lease-leader-election/): Use Kubernetes Lease objects for leader election in controllers and operators. Lease API, client-go leaderelection, lock mechanisms, and HA deployment patterns.

- [Kubernetes Namespaces Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-namespace-guide/): configuration
- [Kubernetes Node and Pod Affinity Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-affinity-guide/): Configure node affinity, pod affinity, and anti-affinity rules for advanced Kubernetes scheduling. Control pod placement across zones, nodes, and topologies.
- [Kubernetes Pod Lifecycle Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-pod-lifecycle/): configuration
- [Kubernetes Pod Lifecycle and States Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-pod-lifecycle-guide/): Understand the Kubernetes pod lifecycle from Pending to Terminated. Covers pod phases, container states, restart policies, graceful shutdown, and preStop hooks.
- [Kubernetes Resource Limits CPU Memory Format](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-resource-limits-cpu-memory-format/): Kubernetes container resource limits and requests syntax. CPU units (200m, 500m, 1), memory units (256Mi, 1Gi), QoS classes, and YAML format examples.
- [Kubernetes Resource Requests and Limits Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-resource-requests-limits/): Configure CPU and memory requests and limits in Kubernetes. Understand QoS classes, OOMKilled, CPU throttling, and right-sizing with VPA recommendations.
- [Kubernetes Scheduler Configuration and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scheduler-configuration-tuning/): Customize the Kubernetes scheduler with scheduling profiles, plugins, and advanced placement strategies for optimal pod placement and resource utilization
- [Kubernetes Sidecar Container Patterns](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-sidecar-patterns/): Implement sidecar containers for logging, proxying, config reload, and security. Built-in sidecar support in Kubernetes 1.28+ with restartPolicy Always.
- [Kubernetes Sidecar Containers Pattern](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-sidecar-containers/): configuration

Separate startup from liveness checks, failureThreshold tuning, and Java/database examples.
- [Kubernetes Taints and Tolerations Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-taint-toleration-guide/): Use Kubernetes taints and tolerations to control pod scheduling. Dedicate nodes for GPU workloads, isolate teams, and prevent scheduling on specific nodes.
- [Kubernetes Taints and Tolerations Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-taint-toleration/): configuration

- [MIG GPU Partitioning with DRA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-mig-partitioning/): Dynamically partition NVIDIA A100 and H100 GPUs using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology with Dynamic Resource Allocation for flexible workload isolation
- [MOFED and DOCA Driver Building for OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mofed-doca-driver-building-openshift/): Build NVIDIA MOFED and DOCA drivers for OpenShift using DriverToolKit, Buildah, and MachineConfig for RDMA and GPU networking.
- [Manage OpenClaw Skills on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-skills-kubernetes/): Deploy and manage OpenClaw agent skills (tools, automations, integrations) on Kubernetes using ConfigMaps, PVCs, and git-sync for dynamic capability.
- [Mixed Accelerator Workloads with DRA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-mixed-accelerators/): Orchestrate heterogeneous accelerator workloads combining GPUs, TPUs, FPGAs, and custom AI chips using Dynamic Resource Allocation
- [Multi-Node NVLink with ComputeDomains](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-computedomains-nvlink/): Configure ComputeDomains for robust and secure Multi-Node NVLink (MNNVL) workloads on NVIDIA GB200 and similar systems using DRA
- [NVIDIA DOCA Driver Container in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/doca-driver-container-kubernetes/): Deploy and configure NVIDIA DOCA Driver containers via NicClusterPolicy for RDMA, NFS-RDMA, and precompiled driver builds.
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator MOFED Driver Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-operator-mofed-driver/): Configure the NVIDIA GPU Operator to deploy Mellanox OFED drivers for high-performance RDMA networking on Kubernetes GPU nodes with InfiniBand and RoCE support.
- [NicClusterPolicy MOFED Affinity and Node Selection](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/nicclusterpolicy-node-selection/): Configure NicClusterPolicy node selectors and affinity rules to deploy MOFED drivers only on RDMA-capable nodes in Kubernetes clusters.
- [Open Kernel Modules and DMA-BUF for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/open-kernel-modules-dma-buf/): Migrate from proprietary NVIDIA kernel modules and nvidia-peermem to open kernel modules with DMA-BUF for safer GPU upgrades.
- [OpenClaw Cron Jobs and Heartbeats on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-cron-heartbeat-kubernetes/): Configure OpenClaw's built-in cron scheduling and heartbeat system on Kubernetes for proactive notifications, periodic checks, and automated background.
- [OpenClaw Multi-Model Provider Setup on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-multi-model-providers-kubernetes/): Configure OpenClaw with multiple AI providers on Kubernetes. Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, OpenRouter with fallback chains and cost control.
- [OpenClaw Node Pairing for IoT and Edge Devices](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-node-pairing-kubernetes/): Pair phones, Raspberry Pi, and edge devices with OpenClaw on Kubernetes. Camera, location, screen control, and remote command execution.
- [OpenShift Lifecycle and Version Support](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-lifecycle-versions/): Understand OpenShift Container Platform version lifecycle, support phases, EUS releases, and upgrade planning for production clusters.
- [OpenShift Project Request Template for Pull Secrets](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-project-request-template/): Configure an OpenShift Project Request Template so every new namespace automatically gets a ServiceAccount with imagePullSecrets for your private Quay registry.
- [OpenShift Upgrade Planning for 2026](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-upgrade-planning-2026/): Plan OpenShift upgrades for 2026. EUS-to-EUS paths, operator compatibility, pre-upgrade checks, canary node pools, and rollback strategy for OCP 4.14 to 4.18.
- [Pause and Unpause MCP Rollouts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-pause-unpause-rollout/): Temporarily pause MachineConfigPool rollouts to batch multiple MachineConfig changes or coordinate with maintenance windows. Unpause to resume node updates.
- [PriorityClasses for GPU Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/priorityclasses-gpu-workloads/): Configure Kubernetes PriorityClasses for GPU workloads with training, serving, batch, and interactive tiers and preemption policies.
- [RHCOS for OpenShift Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/rhcos-openshift-node-management/): Understand and manage Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHCOS) for OpenShift nodes including MachineConfig, ignition, OS updates, and node customization.
- [ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resourcequota-limitrange-gpu/): Configure ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPU workloads with per-tenant caps on GPU, CPU, memory, and object counts in Kubernetes.
- [Restore Scaled Deployments After Node Drain](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/restore-scaled-deployments-after-drain/): Restore deployments scaled down for maintenance. Verify node health, check pod scheduling, and confirm service availability.
- [Scale Deployments to Unblock Node Drains](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scale-deploy-unblock-drain/): Safely scale down deployments that block node drains due to PDB violations. Record original replicas, scale to zero, drain, then restore after the node returns.
- [Separate Worker and Infra MachineConfigPools](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-worker-infra-separation/): Create dedicated MachineConfigPools for infrastructure and GPU nodes. Isolate MCP rollout blast radius and control update order for different node types.
- [Set Kernel Parameters via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-kernel-parameters/): Tune kernel sysctl parameters on OpenShift nodes using MachineConfig. Set networking, memory, and performance sysctls on RHCOS.
- [Switch to Open NVIDIA Kernel Modules on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/switch-proprietary-to-open-kernel-modules/): Step-by-step guide to migrate the NVIDIA GPU Operator from proprietary to open kernel modules on OpenShift, enabling DMA-BUF and GPUDirect Storage support.
- [TPU Allocation with Dynamic Resource Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-tpu-allocation/): Configure Google Cloud TPUs in Kubernetes using DRA for flexible allocation, multi-slice workloads, and optimized machine learning training
- [Tune NCCL Environment Variables for RDMA and Ethernet](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/tune-nccl-env-rdma-ethernet/): Apply safe NCCL environment variable profiles for RDMA-capable and Ethernet-only GPU clusters to maximize collective communication throughput.
- [Use oc adm drain Dry-Run for Diagnostics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/oc-adm-drain-dry-run-diagnostics/): Preview node drain impact without evicting pods. Identify PDB violations, unmanaged pods, and local storage blockers before maintenance.
- [kubectl Cheat Sheet: Essential Commands](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubectl-cheat-sheet/): Complete kubectl cheat sheet with essential commands for pods, deployments, services, logs, debugging, and cluster management. Copy-paste ready examples.
- [kubectl Port-Forward: Access Pods and Services](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-port-forwarding-guide/): Use kubectl port-forward to access Kubernetes pods, services, and deployments from your local machine. Debug, test, and access internal services securely.
- [kubectl create secret docker-registry Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubectl-create-secret-docker-registry/): Create Kubernetes docker-registry secrets with kubectl. Private registry auth, --docker-password-stdin, imagePullSecrets, service account attachment, and YAML format.

## Deployments

- [Air-Gapped Image Import for OpenShift Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/air-gapped-image-import-openshift/): Import container images into disconnected OpenShift clusters. Use podman save/load and internal registries when DNS and TLS block external pulls.
- [Air-Gapped OpenShift with Quay Mirror](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/airgapped-openshift-quay-mirror/): Deploy OpenShift in air-gapped environments with local Quay registry mirror, ImageDigestMirrorSet, and custom CatalogSources.
- [Argo Workflows on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argo-workflows-kubernetes/): deployments
- [ArgoCD App of Apps Pattern](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-pattern/): Implement the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications from a parent Application for cluster bootstrapping.
- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Sync Waves](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-sync-waves/): Combine the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with sync waves to bootstrap entire clusters in dependency order, from CRDs and operators to application workloads.
- [ArgoCD ApplicationSets for Multi-Tenant GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-applicationsets-multi-tenant/): Use ArgoCD ApplicationSets to auto-discover and provision GPU tenant overlays from Git directories with per-tenant sync policies.
- [ArgoCD Declarative Application Setup](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-declarative-application-setup/): Define ArgoCD Applications, Projects, and repository credentials declaratively using Kubernetes manifests for reproducible GitOps configuration.
- [ArgoCD Multi-Cluster App of Apps](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-multi-cluster-app-of-apps/): Manage multiple Kubernetes clusters with ArgoCD App of Apps, deploying shared infrastructure and cluster-specific workloads from a single GitOps repository.
- [ArgoCD PreSync and PostSync Hooks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-presync-postsync-hooks/): Use ArgoCD PreSync hooks for database migrations and PostSync hooks for smoke tests, with SyncFail hooks for automated rollback and cleanup.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for CRD and Operator Ordering](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-crd-operators/): Use ArgoCD sync waves to deploy Custom Resource Definitions before operators and custom resources, preventing CRD race conditions in GitOps pipelines.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Canary Deployments](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-canary/): Use ArgoCD sync waves for canary deployments with Istio traffic splitting, automated validation, and progressive rollout strategies.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Database Migrations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-database-migration/): Use ArgoCD sync waves and PreSync hooks to run database migrations before deploying application code, with rollback strategies.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Ordered Deployments](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-ordering/): Use ArgoCD sync waves to control the order of Kubernetes resource deployment, ensuring dependencies like namespaces and CRDs are created before workloads.
- [Automate NCCL Preflight Checks in CI/CD Pipelines](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/automate-nccl-preflight-ci/): Run NCCL smoke benchmarks automatically in CI/CD pipelines before promoting GPU cluster changes to production, catching regressions early.
- [Backstage Developer Portal on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/backstage-kubernetes-developer-portal/): Deploy Spotify Backstage on Kubernetes as an internal developer portal. Covers Helm install, PostgreSQL backend, catalog entities, and TechDocs integration.
- [Blue-Green Deployment in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-blue-green-deployment/): deployments
- [Blue-Green and Canary Deployments on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-blue-green-canary-deployments/): Implement blue-green and canary deployment strategies with Argo Rollouts and Flagger. Progressive delivery with automated analysis and rollback.
- [Build a Custom OpenClaw Docker Image for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-custom-docker-image/): Create an optimized Docker image for OpenClaw with pre-installed dependencies, custom skills, and workspace files for faster Kubernetes deployments.
- [Build a Kubernetes Operator with Docker Testing](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/build-kubernetes-operator-docker-testing/): Build a Kubernetes operator with Operator SDK and Kubebuilder. Test with Docker, Kind, and envtest. Full TDD workflow to OLM bundle.
- [CNPG Cluster Scaling and Upgrades](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/cnpg-scaling-upgrades/): Scale CloudNativePG clusters, perform rolling PostgreSQL major upgrades, and manage storage expansion without downtime in Kubernetes.
- [Canary Deployment in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-canary-deployment/): deployments
- [CloudNativePG PostgreSQL Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/cnpg-postgresql-operator/): Deploy highly available PostgreSQL clusters on Kubernetes using CloudNativePG operator with automated failover and backups.
- [Cluster API for Kubernetes Lifecycle Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/cluster-api-infrastructure-as-code/): Manage Kubernetes cluster lifecycle with Cluster API. Declarative cluster creation, upgrades, scaling, and multi-cloud infrastructure provisioning as code.
- [Crossplane Kubernetes Infrastructure Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/crossplane-kubernetes-infrastructure/): Manage cloud infrastructure from Kubernetes with Crossplane. Providers, Compositions, Claims, XRDs, and GitOps-driven infrastructure as code for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
- [Data Monetization Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/data-monetization-platform-kubernetes/): Build data monetization platforms on Kubernetes. Data marketplace APIs, usage-based billing, data mesh architecture, secure data sharing, and catalog services.
- [Deploy OpenClaw AI Gateway on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-kubernetes-deployment/): Deploy the OpenClaw multi-channel AI gateway on Kubernetes with persistent storage, TLS ingress, and high availability for WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord.
- [Deploy an OpenClaw Signal Messenger Bot on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-signal-kubernetes/): Run OpenClaw as a Signal messenger AI assistant on Kubernetes with linked device pairing, end-to-end encryption, and persistent sessions.
- [Deploy an OpenClaw Telegram Bot on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-telegram-bot-kubernetes/): Run OpenClaw as a Telegram bot on Kubernetes with BotFather setup, webhook configuration, inline commands, and persistent conversation history.
- [Deployment vs StatefulSet in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/deployment-vs-statefulset/): Choose between Deployment and StatefulSet for your Kubernetes workloads. Compare identity, storage, ordering, scaling, and use cases for each controller.
- [Detect ArgoCD Shadow Updates Out-of-Band](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-shadow-update-detection/): Detect and prevent ArgoCD shadow updates where manual kubectl changes bypass GitOps. Configure self-heal, sync, and drift detection.
- [Enterprise GitOps at Scale with Fleet Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-enterprise-gitops-fleet/): Manage hundreds of Kubernetes clusters with ArgoCD ApplicationSets, Flux multi-cluster, and fleet-wide policy enforcement using GitOps principles.
- [Fix Deployment Rollout Stuck at Partial Progress](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/rollout-stuck-troubleshooting/): Debug deployments stuck with unavailable replicas during rollout. Covers readiness probes, resource constraints, and rollback.
- [Flux vs ArgoCD: Kubernetes GitOps Compared](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/flux-vs-argocd-gitops-comparison/): Compare Flux and ArgoCD for Kubernetes GitOps. Architecture, multi-tenancy, Helm support, UI, scalability, and when to choose each for production GitOps delivery.
- [GPU Operator Canary Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/gpu-operator-upgrade-canary/): Safely upgrade NVIDIA GPU Operator using canary node pools, 48-hour bake periods, validation gates, and Git-based rollback.
- [GitOps Bootstrap for Bare-Metal GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/gitops-bootstrap-bare-metal-gpu/): Bootstrap bare-metal GPU clusters with ArgoCD and Kustomize in air-gapped environments with NVIDIA GPU and Network Operators.
- [GitOps with Flux CD for Continuous Delivery](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/flux-gitops-continuous-delivery/): Implement GitOps workflows using Flux CD to automate Kubernetes deployments, manage infrastructure as code, and maintain desired cluster state from Git.

- [How to Configure Pod Affinity and Anti-Affinity](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-affinity-anti-affinity/): Control pod placement using affinity and anti-affinity rules. Co-locate related pods or spread them across nodes and zones for high availability.
- [How to Configure Pod Disruption Budgets](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-disruption-budget-config/): Protect application availability during voluntary disruptions. Configure PDBs to ensure minimum replicas during node drains, upgrades, and maintenance.
- [How to Configure Pod Lifecycle Hooks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-lifecycle-hooks/): Execute custom actions during pod startup and shutdown with lifecycle hooks. Implement graceful shutdown, initialization tasks, and cleanup operations.
- [How to Configure Pod Priority and Preemption](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-priority-preemption/): Set pod priorities to ensure critical workloads get scheduled first. Configure preemption to evict lower-priority pods when resources are scarce.
- [How to Create and Use Kubernetes Operators](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-operators/): Learn to build Kubernetes Operators for automating application management. Understand custom controllers, the Operator pattern, and frameworks like.
- [How to Deploy Jobs and CronJobs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/jobs-cronjobs/): Master Kubernetes Jobs and CronJobs for batch processing and scheduled tasks. Learn completion modes, parallelism, and failure handling.

- [How to Use Init Containers for Dependencies](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/init-containers/): Master Kubernetes init containers to handle dependencies, setup tasks, and pre-flight checks before your main application starts.
- [How to Use Kubernetes Leases for Leader Election](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-leases/): Implement distributed coordination with Kubernetes Leases. Configure leader election, distributed locks, and high availability patterns.

- [OpenClaw Local Development with Kind](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-kind-local-development/): Set up a local Kind cluster for OpenClaw development and testing. Auto-detect Docker or Podman, create a single-node cluster, and deploy OpenClaw in minutes.
- [OpenClaw Multi-Agent Team Deployment on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-multi-agent-team-kubernetes/): Deploy multiple specialized OpenClaw agents as Kubernetes pods. Dedicated DevOps, security, and writing agents with shared workspace.
- [OpenClaw Multi-Environment Deployment with Kustomize](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-kustomize-overlays/): Deploy OpenClaw across dev, staging, and production Kubernetes environments using Kustomize overlays for configs and secrets.
- [OpenClaw Operator for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-operator-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenClaw AI agents on Kubernetes using the official operator. CRD-based lifecycle, Chromium sidecar, auto-update, and backup.
- [OpenClaw Webhook Automation on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-webhook-automation-kubernetes/): Configure OpenClaw webhooks on Kubernetes for GitHub, Jira, and PagerDuty event-driven automation. Ingress routing, HMAC validation, and hook handler patterns.
- [OpenClaw on OpenShift with SCCs and Routes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-openshift-deployment/): Deploy OpenClaw on OpenShift with Security Context Constraints, Routes for TLS termination, and OpenShift-specific considerations for non-root containers.
- [OpenShift BuildConfig with ImageStream](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-buildconfig-imagestream/): Build container images on OpenShift using BuildConfig with ImageStream triggers, pushing to internal registry or local Quay.
- [OpenShift BuildConfig with Local Quay Registry](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-buildconfig-quay/): Build container images on OpenShift and push to a local Quay registry using BuildConfig, ImageStream, and robot account credentials.
- [OpenShift IDMS and install-config.yaml Mirror Registry](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-idms-install-config/): Configure ImageDigestMirrorSet and install-config.yaml imageContentSources for OpenShift disconnected installations with mirror registries.
- [OpenShift ITMS ImageTagMirrorSet](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-itms-image-tag-mirror/): Configure ImageTagMirrorSet in OpenShift 4.13+ for tag-based image mirroring. Mirror container images by tag instead of digest for disconnected clusters.
- [OpenShift MachineConfigPool After ITMS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-mcp-itms-rollout/): Monitor and manage MachineConfigPool rollouts after applying ImageTagMirrorSet in OpenShift. Handle node restarts, paused pools, and degraded states.
- [OpenShift Serverless KnativeServing](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-serverless-knativeserving/): Deploy and configure OpenShift Serverless Operator with KnativeServing for autoscaling, scale-to-zero, and traffic splitting on Kubernetes.
- [OpenShift Support Lifecycle: Versions, EOL, Upgrades](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openshift-lifecycle-support-versions/): OpenShift support lifecycle with version matrix, EOL dates, EUS releases, and upgrade paths. Complete OCP 4.x lifecycle support guide updated 2026.
- [Platform Engineering on Kubernetes: Build an IDP](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/platform-engineering-internal-developer-platform-kubernetes/): Build an internal developer platform on Kubernetes. Backstage, Crossplane, ArgoCD, self-service templates, golden paths, and platform team patterns for developer experience.
- [Pod Priority and Preemption Scheduling Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-priority-preemption-scheduling/): Control Kubernetes scheduling with Pod Priority and Preemption. Learn to prioritize critical workloads and ensure important pods get scheduled first.
- [Pod Readiness Gates for Custom Conditions](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/pod-readiness-gates/): Implement Pod Readiness Gates to add custom conditions that must be satisfied before a pod is considered ready for traffic, enabling integration with.
- [Push a Podman-Saved Image to Local Quay](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/local-quay-push-podman-image/): Load a Podman image tar archive, tag it for your Local Quay registry, authenticate with robot accounts, and push it safely to your private repo.
- [Quay Robot Accounts for Kubernetes Image Pulls](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/quay-robot-account-kubernetes/): Create Quay robot accounts and configure Kubernetes imagePullSecrets for automated container image pulls from private registries.
- [Retag and Push an Image in Local Quay](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/local-quay-retag-and-push/): Pull an existing container image from Local Quay, retag it for a new repository path or version, and push the updated tag back to the registry.
- [Run NCCL Tests with MPIJob on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/run-nccl-tests-mpijob-kubernetes/): Launch multi-pod NCCL benchmarks using MPIJob on Kubernetes for repeatable, automated distributed GPU communication testing across nodes.
- [Run WebAssembly Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/wasm-workloads-kubernetes/): Deploy WASM workloads on Kubernetes using SpinKube and containerd-shim. Sub-millisecond cold starts, polyglot runtimes, and sandboxed edge computing.
- [Run Windows Containers on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-windows-containers/): Deploy Windows workloads on Kubernetes with mixed Linux and Windows node pools. Covers taints, node selectors, and Windows-specific networking.
- [Run an OpenClaw Discord Bot on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-discord-bot-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenClaw as a Discord bot on Kubernetes with channel routing, mention handling, group chat rules, and persistent conversation memory.
- [Self-Host an OpenClaw WhatsApp AI Assistant on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-whatsapp-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenClaw on Kubernetes to run a personal WhatsApp AI assistant with QR code pairing, persistent sessions, media support, and allow-list security.
- [Serverless Functions with WebAssembly on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/wasm-serverless-functions-kubernetes/): Build serverless functions using WebAssembly on Kubernetes with Fermyon Cloud, KEDA, and SpinKube. Achieve sub-millisecond scale-to-zero with Wasm cold starts.
- [Tekton Pipelines on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/tekton-pipelines-kubernetes/): deployments
- [Topology Spread Constraints for HA Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/topology-spread-constraints/): Distribute pods across nodes, zones, and regions using topology spread constraints. Ensure high availability and fault tolerance for your workloads.
- [WASI and containerd Wasm Shims on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/wasm-wasi-containerd-kubernetes/): Run WebAssembly workloads using containerd Wasm shims with WASI support on Kubernetes. Configure runwasi, wasmtime, and WasmEdge as container runtimes.
- [WebAssembly Runtime on Kubernetes with Spin and SpinKube](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/wasm-runtime-kubernetes/): Deploy WebAssembly workloads on Kubernetes using SpinKube and the Spin Operator. Run Wasm components alongside containers with sub-millisecond cold starts.
- [Zero-Downtime Deployment in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-zero-downtime-deployment/): deployments

## Helm

- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Helm Values](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/argocd-app-of-apps-helm-values/): Use the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with Helm value overrides per environment, enabling templated Application manifests and DRY multi-environment configurations.
- [Fix Helm Upgrade Failed and Rollback](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-upgrade-failed-troubleshooting/): Debug failed Helm releases stuck in pending-upgrade or failed state. Covers atomic upgrades, manual rollback, secret storage cleanup, and history limits.
