- [How to Troubleshoot Kubernetes Networking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-network-troubleshooting/): Debug and resolve Kubernetes networking issues systematically. Learn to diagnose DNS problems, service connectivity, network policies, and CNI issues.
- [How to Use Ephemeral Containers for Debugging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/ephemeral-containers-debugging/): Debug running pods using ephemeral containers without restarting. Learn kubectl debug techniques for troubleshooting production workloads.
- [ITMS Race Condition with Ingress Controllers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/itms-ingress-controller-race-condition/): Resolve the ITMS race condition where ImageTagMirrorSet rollouts deadlock with hostNetwork ingress controllers during MCO drain.
- [Identify Mellanox Interface Models from Linux and PCI Data](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/identify-mellanox-nic-models/): Map interface names to PCI addresses and Mellanox model generations to build accurate SR-IOV policies and GPU networking configurations on Kubernetes.
- [Kubernetes Debugging Toolkit and Commands](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-debugging-toolkit/): Essential kubectl debugging commands and tools for Kubernetes troubleshooting. Covers ephemeral containers, debug pods, network debugging, and log analysis.
- [Kubernetes Ephemeral Containers: kubectl debug](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-ephemeral-containers-debug/): Use kubectl debug and ephemeral containers to troubleshoot running pods. Attach debug containers without restarting, inspect distroless images, and node debugging.
- [Kubernetes Pod Eviction: Causes and Prevention](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-pod-eviction/): Understand why Kubernetes evicts pods and how to prevent it. Covers resource pressure, priority classes, PDBs, and eviction policies.
- [Kubernetes Troubleshooting Flowchart](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-troubleshooting-guide/): troubleshooting
- [MCP Drain Blocked by PDB: Workaround](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/mcp-drain-pdb-workaround/): Resolve OpenShift MachineConfigPool drain failures caused by PodDisruptionBudget violations. Scale down and restore after update.
- [Node Drain Blocked by hostNetwork Port Conflicts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/node-drain-hostnetwork-ports/): Debug and fix OpenShift node drains that fail because hostNetwork pods cannot schedule replacements due to port exhaustion across the cluster.
- [OOMKilled in Kubernetes: How to Debug and Fix](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/debug-oom-killed/): Fix OOMKilled errors in Kubernetes pods. Learn why containers get OOMKilled (exit code 137), how to set memory limits, debug memory leaks, and prevent OOM.
- [OpenShift Ingress Router Troubleshooting](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/openshift-ingress-router-troubleshooting/): Debug OpenShift HAProxy router issues: pods stuck Pending, hostPort conflicts, PDB violations during maintenance, and custom router deployment scaling problems.
- [OpenShift crun vs runc Runtime Differences](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/openshift-crun-runc-runtime-differences/): Understand why pods behave differently on GPU vs CPU nodes in OpenShift. Compare crun and runc container runtimes, seccomp profiles, and syscall filtering.
- [OpenShift oc debug Mount Limitation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/openshift-debug-mount-limitation/): Why NFS and filesystem mounts via oc debug node disappear after the debug pod exits. Understand the container namespace isolation and use MachineConfig instead.
- [PDB Allowed Disruptions Zero: Debugging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/pdb-allowed-disruptions-zero/): Debug PodDisruptionBudgets stuck at zero allowed disruptions. Understand minAvailable vs maxUnavailable, fix eviction failures, and plan for maintenance.
- [SR-IOV VF Troubleshooting on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/sriov-vf-troubleshooting/): Diagnose and fix SR-IOV Virtual Function issues including VF creation failures, device plugin errors, RDMA problems, and network attachment failures.
- [Troubleshoot CatalogSource and OLM Issues](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/openshift-catalogsource-troubleshooting/): Debug CatalogSource failures including pod crashes, gRPC errors, stale caches, and operator install problems in OpenShift OLM environments.
- [Troubleshoot NVIDIA NIM TensorRT-LLM Initialization Failures](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-nim-tensorrt-llm/): Diagnose and fix common NIM TensorRT-LLM executor failures including DecoderState mismatch, version incompatibilities, and engine build errors.
- [Troubleshoot nv-ipam 'Pool Not Found' Errors in Multus](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-nv-ipam-pool-not-found/): Fix nv-ipam IPPool lookup failures in Multus by aligning SriovNetwork, NetworkAttachmentDefinition, and IPPool names and namespaces correctly.
- [Troubleshoot nvidia-fs Module Conflict on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/troubleshoot-nvidia-fs-module-conflict/): Diagnose and fix the 'insmod: ERROR: could not insert module nvidia-fs.ko: File exists' error when enabling GPUDirect Storage with the NVIDIA GPU Operator.
- [Troubleshooting Pending PersistentVolumeClaims](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-pending-pvc/): Diagnose and fix PVCs stuck in Pending status. Learn common causes including StorageClass issues, capacity problems, and node affinity conflicts with.
- [Troubleshooting Pods with GPU Devices on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-pods-devices-gpu-kubernetes/): Fix GPU device issues in Kubernetes pods. Troubleshoot device plugin errors, DRA claims, CUDA failures, driver mismatches, and GPU scheduling problems step by step.
- [Validate GPU and NIC Topology Before NCCL Benchmarks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/validate-gpu-topology-nccl/): Inspect node-level GPU, NIC, and PCI topology on Kubernetes workers to predict and explain NCCL benchmark performance before running tests.
- [Validate SR-IOV Operator Health Across Multiple Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/validate-sriov-on-multiple-nodes/): Run a full checklist to confirm SR-IOV discovery, VF creation, scheduler resources, and pod attachment on multiple nodes.
- [kubectl exec: Run Commands Inside Kubernetes Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/troubleshooting/kubernetes-exec-into-pod/): Use kubectl exec to run commands inside Kubernetes pods. Covers interactive sessions, multi-container pods, and ephemeral container debugging.
